channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biggest November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds flights
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The biggest November snowstorm to hit South Korea’s capital in more than a half century blanketed the capital on Wednesday, grounding hundreds of flights, disrupting commuter traffic and leaving at least two dead.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Namibia votes and could have its first female leader. But election upsets have shaken the region
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — A 72-year-old woman who joined Namibia's independence movement in the 1970s is a strong contender to become its first female leader as the country votes Wednesday in a presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests arrest warrants for the head of Myanmar's military regime
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has asked judges to issue an arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar’s military regime for crimes committed against the country’s Rohingya Muslim minority.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire appears to hold and Lebanese begin streaming south to their homes
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah appeared to be holding, as residents in cars heaped with belongings streamed back toward southern Lebanon despite warnings from the Israeli and Lebanese military that they stay away.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb And Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah start a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants began a ceasefire Wednesday in a major step toward ending nearly 14 months of fighting as a region on edge wondered whether it will hold.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Displaced people return to south Lebanon as ceasefire appears to hold
item.description: Long-displaced residents of south Lebanon have started returning to their homes after a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group took effect early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you protect children from the harms of social media? Politically, the answer appears simple in Australia, as legislation tackling the problem moves quickly through the Parliament. Practically, however, the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Carol Zeghayer gripped her IV as she hurried down the brightly lit hallway of Beirut’s children’s cancer center. The 9-year-old's face brightened when she spotted her playmates from the oncology ward.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU AL JOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 arrested in Laos after 6 tourists died from suspected methanol poisoning
item.description: Officials arrested eight hostel workers in Laos on Monday for allegedly poisoning travelers with methanol, according to reports from the Australian Broadcasting Corp. Tourists reported illnesses after consuming alcohol in the town of Vang Vieng, a popular backpacking destination.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia expels British diplomat after accusing him of spying
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian authorities on Tuesday ordered a British diplomat to leave the country on allegations of spying that the U.K. dismissed as “baseless” as tensions soar over the conflict in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 6 dead as protests erupt in Pakistan over jailed former Prime Minister Imran Khan
item.description: Six people were killed as protests supporting imprisoned former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan escalated in Islamabad on Tuesday, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation was ordered detained on charges of sedition Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top EU diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Congress looking to put suffocating sanctions on 'kangaroo' ICC over Netanyahu arrest warrant
item.description: After the ICC’s legal assault on the Mideast’s only democracy, Israel, for its war on Hamas terrorism, a powerful U.S. legislative and executive campaign has emerged against the controversial body.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 16 tourists missing after Egyptian yacht carrying Americans sinks in high-waves Red Sea
item.description: A tourist boat called the Sea Story sank in the Red Sea on Monday near the resort town of Marsa Alam. Sixteen people are missing, including twelve foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rabbi feared kidnapped, killed by terrorists after going missing, prompting investigation
item.description: Rabbi Zvi Kogan, a Chabad emissary, is feared dead after "terrorist involvement" was found related to his disappearance Thursday. Israeli and Emirati officials are investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9
